findings.evidence: Basic.lean:115 `@[grind →] lemma contDiffOn`, Basic.lean:120 `@[grind =>] lemma neg_one_pow_mul_iteratedDerivWithin_nonneg`, Basic.lean:137 `@[grind =>] lemma nonneg`, Basic.lean:143 `@[grind =>] lemma derivWithin_nonpos`; the new file's corresponding lemmas have no attribute lines.
findings.fix: Annotate the open-half-line and Bernstein projections to mirror Basic.lean: @[grind →] on the contDiffOn projections, @[grind =>] on nonneg, neg_one_pow_mul_iteratedDeriv_nonneg, deriv_nonpos, and IsBernsteinFunction.deriv_nonneg (and deriv_isCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi as the main elimination).
findings.issue: Characteristic projection lemmas carry no @[grind] annotations, while their exact parallels in Basic.lean do: IsCompletelyMonotone.contDiffOn is @[grind →], and .nonneg / .neg_one_pow_mul_iteratedDerivWithin_nonneg / .derivWithin_nonpos are @[grind =>]. The new IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi.{contDiffOn, nonneg, neg_one_pow_mul_iteratedDeriv_nonneg, deriv_nonpos} and IsBernsteinFunction.{nonneg, deriv_nonneg, deriv_isCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi} are the same kind of characteristic lemma but drive nothing.

summary: Surface is minimal and bodies are hidden, and the characteristic API for both new predicates is complete. But the new projection lemmas omit the @[grind] annotations that the directly-parallel lemmas in the sibling Basic.lean carry, leaving downstream automation inconsistent.
